text:
SUMMARY

This is AI generated summarization, which may have errors. For context, always refer to the full article.

MANILA, Philippines – Medals have been elusive for Team Philippines through the first week of the Paris Olympics, but that could all change on Saturday, August 3.

Here is the schedule (Manila time):

For the second straight edition, a Filipino captures an Olympic gold medal.

Carlos Yulo delivers the Philippines’ first-ever medal in Olympic gymnastics as he rules the men’s floor exercise final in the Paris Games.

Yulo nets 15.000 points to dethrone Israel’s Artem Dolgopyat, who settles for second with 14.966 points.

Top qualifier Jake Jarman of Great Britain bags bronze with 14.933 points.

No medal repeat for Carlo Paalam in the Olympics as he falls to Australia’s Charlie Senior, 3-2, in the quarterfinals of the men’s 57kg division.

Senior narrowly escapes by scores of 30-27, 29-28, 29-28, 27-30, 28-29.

Only Nesthy Petecio and Aira Villegas are in the running for the Philippine boxing team.

Read the full storyhere.

Carlos Yulo puts himself in gold-medal contention as he takes the lead through the first four qualifiers in the men’s floor exercise final with 15.000 points.

Reigning Olympic champion Artem Dolgopyat of Israel is second with 14.966 points.

EJ Obiena claims his spot in the men’s pole vault final in the Paris Olympics after finishing in the top 12 of the qualification.

Ranked second in the world, Obiena clears 5.75m as he gets a shot at winning Philippine athletics’ first Olympic medal since 1936.

Sweden’s Armand Duplantis headlines the finalists as he aims to defend his Olympic throne.

Read the full storyhere.

EJ Obiena easily clears 5.75m in a single attempt as he inches closer to a spot in the men’s pole vault final.

Obiena has found his groove as 14 remain in contention.

EJ Obiena lets out a loud roar out of relief as he hurdles 5.70m after back-to-back fouls at 5.60m.

Obiena needs to clear 5.80m to advance to the final.

EJ Obiena skips 5.60m and will have one attempt to surpass 5.70m and stay in the hunt for a final spot.

EJ Obiena on the brink of elimination as he fails to clear 5.60m on his second attempt.

Another foul and he is out.

EJ Obiena commits a foul on his first attempt at 5.60m as he hits the bar on his way up.

He has two more tries to advance to the next height.

EJ Obiena passes on 5.40m together with Sweden’s Armand Duplantis and Greece’s Emmanouil Karalis.

The next height is 5.60m.

Nesthy Petecio dominates the last two rounds to dispose of home bet Amina Zidani of France and advance to the quarterfinals of the women’s 57kg, claiming a 4-1 win.

The four judges score it 30-27, 29-28, 29-28, 29-28 in favor of Petecio, while the other gives it to Zidani, 29-28.

Petecio, who won silver in Tokyo, is a victory away from repeating as an Olympic medalist.

Read the full storyhere.

GymnastCarlos Yuloand boxerCarlo Paalamlead the medal charge on a star-studded day also featuring pole vaulterEJ Obienaand boxerNesthy Petecio.

Yulo shoots for the Philippines’ first-ever Olympic gymnastics medal as he competes in the men’s floor exercise final, which includes top qualifier Jake Jarman of Great Britain and defending champion Artem Dolgopyat of Israel.

Paalam, meanwhile, aims to assure himself of at least a bronze when he battles Australia’s Charlie Senior in the men’s 57kg quarterfinal.

Obiena begins his medal quest as he sees action in the men’s pole vault qualification, hoping to clear the 5.80-meter qualifying standard or be among the 12 best performers to reach the final.

Petecio resumes her bid of repeating as an Olympic medalist, facing France’s Amina Zidani in the round of 16 of the women’s 57kg division.– Rappler.com

title: HIGHLIGHTS, RESULTS: Team Philippines, 2024 Paris Olympics – August 3
author: delfin.dioquino editor
description: Gymnast Carlos Yulo and boxer Carlo Paalam lead the medal charge on a star-studded day also featuring pole vaulter EJ Obiena and boxer Nesthy Petecio
